headline: Healthcare Leads a Fractured Session as Semiconductors Take a Brutal Hit
body_html:
Wednesday's session split cleanly along sector lines, with Healthcare surging 3.17% to claim the top spot while the broader tape finished with modest positive breadth — 109 names advancing against 88 declining across a 198-name universe. Consumer and Homebuilder names added to the constructive tone, rising 1.91% and 1.03% respectively, suggesting investors rotated toward domestically oriented, defensive growth even as geopolitical unease — centered on Iran's warnings against military strikes ahead of funeral processions for the late supreme leader — kept risk appetite measured.

The session's sharpest rotation punished semiconductor and materials names without mercy. The chip sector shed 6.5% as a group, with memory, testing-equipment, packaging, and process-materials names all dropping double digits. Sandisk led the carnage with a 14.1% decline, followed closely by Teradyne at 13.6% and Amkor Technology at 12.9%. Corning, straddling the materials and tech-supply-chain worlds, fell 10.8%, extending the pain beyond pure-play semis.

The standout story of the day sat firmly in financial-data. Equifax and S&P Global both surged past 6%, with Fair Isaac not far behind at 5.3%, making Financial-Data the most concentrated pocket of strength outside Healthcare. When credit-scoring and analytics names move in lockstep like that, it typically signals a repricing of confidence in economic data quality and financial intermediation — a quiet but telling message from the market heading into the holiday-shortened week.

briefs.headline: Financial-Data Names Dominate the Gainers Board
briefs.body: Equifax, S&P Global, and Fair Isaac each advanced more than 5% on Wednesday, making Financial-Data the most concentrated pocket of outperformance in the session. The synchronized move suggests investors are placing renewed confidence in companies that sit at the intersection of credit analytics and capital-markets intelligence.

briefs.headline: Semiconductor Rout Drags Entire Supply Chain Lower
briefs.body: The semiconductor sector dropped 6.5% as a whole, with losses cascading from memory and packaging names all the way into process-materials suppliers. The breadth of the decline — spanning five distinct semiconductor sub-industries — points to a macro concern rather than any single company-specific catalyst.

briefs.headline: Intuitive Surgical Lifts Healthcare Devices into the Spotlight
briefs.body: Intuitive Surgical climbed nearly 5.9%, helping propel Healthcare to the best sector performance of the session at 3.17%. The robotic-surgery specialist's move reinforced a broader bid for medical-technology names, which benefited from defensive rotation amid unsettled geopolitical headlines out of the Middle East.

briefs.headline: Kuwait Ramps Crude Output Following US-Iran Arrangement
briefs.body: Kuwait sharply increased crude production in June after a reported US-Iran agreement, according to a sourced account Wednesday. The supply development weighed on the narrative around oil scarcity even as geopolitical risk premiums remain elevated following unrest in Syria and the ongoing mourning period in Iran — leaving Energy flat on the day, down just 0.04%.

briefs.headline: Apple Closes Among the Session's Strongest Performers
briefs.body: Apple finished up 4.84%, placing it among the top six gainers across the full universe and offering a rare bright spot for large-cap technology on an otherwise punishing day for hardware-adjacent chip names. The divergence between Apple and the broader semiconductor complex underscored how selectively capital moved within the tech ecosystem.

briefs.headline: Roche Advances as Overseas Pharma Attracts Defensive Bids
briefs.body: Swiss pharmaceutical giant Roche gained 5.48%, reflecting investor appetite for large-cap international pharma as a defensive shelter. The move aligned with the Healthcare sector's broad outperformance and suggests cross-border diversification remained an active theme as traders weighed Middle East instability and a mixed domestic economic backdrop.
